name: draw_sketch
description: Draw a line-art sketch to the screen using simulated pen input, for Draw-button mode. Unlike draw_svg, you must also report whether the input selection was already a drawing, since that changes where the app places your sketch: into the same box as the original (replacing it) vs. into a fresh box below it.

selection_is_drawing.description: true if the input selection was already mostly a drawing/sketch/diagram (so your svg redraws/replaces it in the SAME spot); false if the input was mostly text (so your svg is a new illustrative doodle placed in a fresh box near it). Get this right -- it controls placement.

svg.description: SVG line-art to be rendered. This should be sized 768x1024 px and roughly centered/composed within that canvas -- the app measures your ink and scales/positions it into the correct box for you, so do not compute placement coordinates yourself. The display can only show black and white. Use fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" for all lines/shapes -- never use fill; use short parallel hatching lines instead of fill for any shading/texture. Keep every stroke a clean, continuous, single-weight contour -- no jagged, sketchy, or broken linework, since this is traced into real pen strokes and messy paths render poorly. Do not use a style tag, gradients, transparency, or shadows. Do include the xmlns in the main svg tag. How much detail/line count to use depends on the case described in the prompt -- follow that, not a fixed rule here.
